generator_prompt_injection:
REAL TEST-TAKER INSIGHTS (sourced from r/ACT, updated 2026-06-10):
- Coverage: Balanced coverage — neither overly wide nor deep.
- High-emphasis topics (per real students): writing, english, math, reading, science.
- Students struggle most with: math, science. Generate proportionally more on these.
- Wording style: ~0% of items should use realistic exam-style indirection (NOT unfair ambiguity).
- Difficulty target: 30% easy / 50% medium / 20% hard. avg pass score 25 suggests easier test.
- Authenticity bar: students compare us to ACT, Erica Meltzer ACT English book, PrepPros Enhanced ACT Math prep book. Match that register.

popup_tips.text: Heaviest-weighted topics on the actual ACT ENGLISH exam (per recent test-takers): writing, english, math. Spend more time here.
popup_tips.source_attribution: Compiled from real test-takers on r/ACT

popup_tips.text: Where ACT ENGLISH students reported struggling most: math, science. Make sure you can handle these before exam day.

popup_tips.text: Resources real ACT ENGLISH test-takers credited: ACT and Erica Meltzer ACT English book. Pair with practice questions.

popup_tips.text: A passing score on ACT ENGLISH is reachable: recent successful test-takers reported scoring around 25 after focused study.
